design_philosophy.core_loop: Select historical fighter, compete in tournament bracket against AI and other players, progress through increasingly difficult opponents
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Mastering character-specific move sets and reading opponent patterns in head-to-head combat
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Control iconic historical and fictional warriors in time-displaced combat tournament
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Simplified 3-button control scheme (punch, kick, throw) with hold-to-strengthen mechanics departs from standard 6-button fighting game layout; emphasis on Death Match mode with environmental hazards rather than pure skill-based combat

difficulty.lives_system: Standard fighting game health bar per round; best-of-3 or best-of-5 round format
difficulty.checkpointing: Tournament bracket progression; advancement through defeating sequential opponents
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character selection allows playstyle variance; Death Match mode introduces environmental RNG that can aid or hinder player performance

progression.unlock_mechanism: Tournament advancement unlocks subsequent opponent matchups; character roster selection available from start
replayability_hooks: Multiple playable characters with distinct move sets
Death Match mode with random environmental hazards
Two-player versus mode for competitive play
Different tournament paths based on character selection

world_building_techniques: Character selection screen with historical/fictional fighter identities
Time-machine tournament premise establishing cross-era combat justification
Stage selection reflecting character origins (historical periods and locations)

notable_bosses: Final tournament opponent varies by character selected

faction_indicators: Historical period identity of selected fighter
danger_signals: Death Match mode glorification of hazard-based combat environment
environmental_storytelling: Stage designs reflect character historical context
Environmental hazards in Death Match (spiked walls, landmines, burning ropes, slick floors) create arena-specific gameplay differentiation

sources_used: Wikipedia: World Heroes article - Gameplay section, Story section, List of games in the series
